---
description: 'Use when adding prayers, liturgical texts, or devotional formulas to any markdown document. Enforces proper line-by-line formatting with appropriate line breaks.'
applyTo: '**/*.md'
---

# Prayer Formatting Guidelines

When including prayers, liturgical texts, or devotional formulas in markdown documents, format them with **proper line breaks** so each line of the prayer stands on its own, preserving the poetic and devotional structure.

## Do

Format prayers with each phrase on its own line, indented for visual clarity:

    O my God, I am heartily sorry for having offended Thee,
    and I detest all my sins because of Thy just punishments,
    but most of all because they offend Thee, my God,
    who art all good and deserving of all my love.
    I firmly resolve, with the help of Thy grace,
    to sin no more and to avoid the near occasions of sin. Amen.

## Don't

Do not wrap prayers in blockquotes (`>`) that collapse them into a single paragraph:

> O my God, I am heartily sorry for having offended Thee, and I detest all my sins because of Thy just punishments, but most of all because they offend Thee, my God, who art all good and deserving of all my love. I firmly resolve, with the help of Thy grace, to sin no more and to avoid the near occasions of sin. Amen.

Do not run prayer lines together as a single paragraph without breaks.

## Additional Notes

- Use **indentation** (4 spaces or a code block) for the prayer text to distinguish it from surrounding prose.
- If the prayer has a Latin original or an Eastern-rite variant, present it with the same line-break treatment.
- For very short prayers (e.g., "Angel of God, my guardian dear…"), a single line is acceptable.
- Prefer **consistent line breaks** matching the natural rhythm of the prayer — each clause or petition on its own line.
